description: Failing run preserved alongside agent-v1. Four failures surfaced real system bugs: scope defaulting on empty caller_id, escalation regex coverage gap, LLM non-determinism on keyword matching, and missing pre-RAG empathy gate. Contact center quality management heritage: bad calls are analyzed, not buried.

failures.root_cause: Empty string caller_id is falsy in Python but was not handled consistently with null. Scope defaulted to authorized instead of public.
Escalation regex did not cover regulatory complaint patterns. The pre-RAG escalation detector missed a category that the corpus contained content for.

LLM non-determinism. The model sometimes uses 'hardship' and sometimes uses synonyms like 'financial difficulty' or 'assistance program'. The expected_contains assertion is brittle against valid rephrasings.

failures.symptom: Expected tier_0 response >20 chars, got tier_2 (78 chars)
failures.root_cause: Distress signal in message ('I just lost my mother') triggered fail-closed RAG path, but the query was account-related and should have received a tier_0 empathy response before retrieval. Missing pre-RAG empathy gate.

cost_fields.reason: DB password not passed to eval runner

heritage_mapping.ENG-066: Scope enforcement gap: IVR would have rejected an empty caller ID at the front door
heritage_mapping.ENG-070: Routing gap: IVR category table missing an entry, calls fall through to default
heritage_mapping.ENG-075: QM sampling variance: same agent, different phrasing across calls, within tolerance
heritage_mapping.ENG-078: Missing IVR front-end screening: distress signal should route before reaching the knowledge base
